description: Every open question resolves to the restrictive reading. (a) An unresolved mandatory gate under the default fail_closed timeout policy terminates the run failed (never silently approves). (b) A command lacking its grant, and an Enqueue whose RunSpec would LOOSEN the floor, are DENIED; the denial lives ONLY in the Control-Plane audit ledger and never fabricates a run-stream event. (c) Cross-tenant access is default-deny and cross-tenant co-residency forces isolation_profile >= strict, with no auto-downgrade.
conventions: canonical proto3 JSON. 'mustReject' entries must fail enqueue/validation. enum-0 restrictiveness is asserted globally at the end.

termination.detail: gate g-deploy timed out under fail_closed after max_wait

mustReject_enqueue_loosening_floor.description: Mission layer tries to loosen the org floor: org mandates isolation profile_floor HARDENED, mission asks for STANDARD; and org mandates a fail_closed gate that mission flips to fail_open.

authDecision.reason: cross-tenant default-deny (caller tenant alpha != run tenant beta)

enumZeroRestrictiveness.TERMINAL_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: read as FAILED, invalid to emit

assertions.assert: scenario_a: with timeoutPolicy TIMEOUT_POLICY_FAIL_CLOSED and no gate.resolved before max_wait, the run terminates TERMINAL_STATE_FAILED with reason TERMINAL_REASON_GATE_REJECTED — an unresolved gate never silently passes
scenario_a: a gate.resolved carrying GATE_DECISION_UNSPECIFIED (value 0) is treated as GATE_DECISION_REJECT

scenario_b: ResolveGate by a principal without PERMISSION_GATES_RESOLVE yields AUTHZ_OUTCOME_DENY recorded ONLY as a ControlPlaneAuditLedgerEnvelope/AuthDecision; NO run-stream event (e.g. gate.resolved) is emitted for the denied call

scenario_b: the enqueue whose mission layer loosens the org floor (isolation profile_floor STANDARD < org HARDENED; gate flipped to fail_open; egress DENY->ALLOW) is REJECTED at Enqueue (grpc error, empty runId); composition is profile=max/egress=intersection/resource=min so a lower layer cannot loosen, and the intrinsic floor is enforced by enqueue-reject, never silent correction
RunSpec declares no consumer name and no callback/webhook/*_url field (structural): Arkwen never calls out; the dependency is one-way consumer->Arkwen
scenario_c: a runs:read by a principal in tenant 'alpha' against a run in tenant 'beta' yields AUTHZ_OUTCOME_DENY (cross-tenant default-deny); recorded only in the audit ledger

scenario_c: cross-tenant co-residency requires isolation_profile >= ISOLATION_PROFILE_STRICT; if the host cannot satisfy strict, provisioning fails closed with TERMINAL_REASON_ISOLATION_UNSATISFIABLE (a reason on FAILED, never a new state, never an auto-downgrade)

global enum-0 restrictiveness holds: unset TimeoutPolicy==FAIL_CLOSED, GATE_DECISION_UNSPECIFIED->REJECT, unset AuthzOutcome==DENY, unset EgressAction==DENY, ISOLATION_PROFILE_UNSPECIFIED->reject, TERMINAL_STATE_UNSPECIFIED->FAILED
